entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 25 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 25 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-25 07:33AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Manly players threaten to boycott game over club’s gay pride jersey
  • WA Police seek public’s help in Narrows Bridge mystery
  • Australia news: Politicians return to Canberra ahead of parliament’s return; farmers back calls to keep Indonesia border open amid foot and mouth disease outbreak
  • Man guilty of advocating terrorism to be released from jail
  • Key Collingwood figures hit out at Ginnivan treatment; triple-premiership Tiger calls time
  • Truckie appeals 22-year sentence for killing four police officers in crash
  • Monkeypox is now a public health emergency. Is the virus changing?
  • Sydney councils in fierce battle over future home of SBS
  • What the worst park in Melbourne says about our pandemic sacrifices
  • Best of cartoons, July 25, 2022
  • Metro Trains feared shortage of vaccinated staff would derail network
  • You think you know opera? Victorian Opera’s new AD will challenge you on that
  • Can the Tigers overturn a result? An in-depth look at 90 seconds of chaos
  • EML Payments shares plunge as Irish central bank finds ‘shortcomings’
  • Morrison to miss first week of new parliament
  • ‘Terminated without notice’: Amazon’s gig platform challenged over driver ‘sacking’
  • PM’s ‘decent’ aspirations may collide with heat of political battle
  • Consumer spending starts to dry up as interest rates rise
  • Government sparks fresh battle after scrapping building watchdog’s ‘ridiculous’ powers
  • I was trying to shut down a culture war, Bill Shorten says
  • Best of cartoons, July 24, 2022
  • The latest illustrations from artist Alan Moir
  • PM promises respect and question time reform – but Dorothy Dixers will stay
  • Sick pay for casuals ‘giving up’, as government targets security over conditions
  • PM backs ‘strongest ever’ biosecurity measures as farmers, business say keep Indonesia border open
  • Morrison government’s election-day tactics crossed the line
  • ‘Porn king’ employee to be resentenced after some stealing convictions overturned
  • ‘I’m being intruded on and harassed’: Barilaro speaks after scuffle with cameraman
  • Thick fog and technical issue cause major delays at Sydney Airport
  • Damage to new Manly ferries to worsen, leaked report says
  • ‘We’re different’: northern beaches resident groups defeat cycleways plan
  • Hundreds of NSW children paid off COVID-19 fines through ‘work orders’
  • Generations of generosity when it’s all in the family
  • From the Archives, 1972: Behind the ping-pong ball
  • ‘We’ve taken our time’: Stokes gives green light to e-scooter trial
  • Possibly not the Daptoids you are looking for
  • Wild weather construction delays to keep beloved Sydney beach closed all summer
  • German chancellor and wife criticised after foxes scatter confidential papers
  • Myanmar junta executes democracy activists, the first such killings in decades
  • Robot breaks child’s finger during chess game
  • Russia tells famine-fearing Africa it’s not to blame for food shortage
  • On the campaign trail, many Republicans see a civil war
  • First order of business: Caroline Kennedy, senior US envoy to visit Solomon Islands
  • Russian aggression an opportunity for faster switch to renewable energy, says COP president
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • French blame Brexit as UK travellers wait in cars for more than 7 hours to cross border
  • Random acts of clickbait have little to do with kindness
  • Bulk carrier crewman pulled from water off WA coast; search continues for missing fisherman
  • Pets allowed: Government urged to adopt sweeping reforms giving WA renters more power
  • Premier warns at-risk COVID groups ‘consider not going’ to the Ekka
  • Drunk, unlicensed boat captain killed daughter-in-law in Brisbane River crash
  • ‘Bigger knolls at Bondi’: Ben Roberts-Smith barrister rejects cliff kick claim
  • Airfares hit maximum altitude as capacity constraints hit the market
  • Crown’s Sydney casino set to open for gaming on August 8
  • COVID-19, supply chain disruptions bite miners as cost pressures rise
  • The US is setting up the RBA for some painful decisions
  • The Wrap: ASX falls flat after 20-day high amid tech slide
  • Travellers unfazed by COVID numbers as Flight Centre sees clear skies
  • Elon Musk accused of affair with Google co-founder’s wife
  • Mining activity in WA protected forests, reserves approved 33 times in 5 years
  • The NFT craze is coming to retailers near you
  • Net-zero doubles for top 200 firms, but investors want more detail
  • Curbing use of market power can help fight against inflation
  • ABC, Betoota Advocate and Dr Seuss for my four year old: the media habits of Annabelle Williams
  • Meet the masked men behind Brown Cardigan who specialise in viral humour
  • Victorian hydro scheme to play bigger role in green power shift
  • Australia needs insurance against future inflation
  • ‘I prayed for an audition’: Aussie star Sam Reid follows in Tom Cruise’s footsteps
  • It’s bonkers and blasphemous, but there’s lots to like in Adam and Eve update
  • Stan Grant named as new sole host of Q+A
  • Margot, Delta, Guy and beyond: What to expect from the Neighbours finale
  • Wendy Whiteley’s second gift: ‘The time has come to give women a bit of attention’
  • Could a smaller, bolder Splendour be a better festival experience?
  • Extinction looms for two vulnerable Australian marine species
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 25 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Myanmar junta executes four pro-democracy activists, state media says
  • Debris from China rocket launch to crash-land — and no one knows where
  • In Okinawa, a push to revive a lost tattoo art for women, by women
  • At least 17 Haitian migrants dead after Miami-bound boat capsizes off Bahamas
  • The problem with ‘great power competition’
  • Two dead in Whistler after daylight shooting in Canadian resort town
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Russia blames West for global food crisis despite blockade on Ukrainian grain
  • Diana Kennedy, cookbook author who promoted Mexican cuisine, dies at 99
  • Western Japan volcano, Sakurajima, erupts; alert level raised
  • Pakistani election upset favors Imran Khan’s return to power
  • His city occupied, a Ukrainian mayor fights to save his community
  • Young Ukrainians use techno parties to rebuild villages
  • China adds science laboratory to its orbiting space station
  • 'The money is gone': Evacuated Ukrainians forced to return
  • Volcanic eruption in Japan forces evacuations in 2 towns
  • Pope set for historic apology for school abuses in Canada
  • Boat carrying Haitian migrants sinks off Bahamas, killing 17
  • Pope lands in Canada, set for apologies to Indigenous groups
  • Bus falls off a bridge in Kenya, leaves 21 dead
  • Fire damages homes in southern Greece; more blazes active
  • Diana Kennedy, food writer devoted to Mexico, dies at 99
  • ‘The stakes could not be higher’: Kansas abortion vote set for Aug. 2
  • Heat indexes soar above 100 in the Northeast, and it’s not over yet
  • 9-year-old survived Iowa campground attack that killed family, mayor says
  • Claim that sex ed ‘grooms’ kids jolted Nebraska politics a year before it swept the nation
  • The Claremont Institute triumphed in the Trump years. Then came Jan. 6.
  • Jan. 6 committee could ‘contemplate a subpoena’ for Ginni Thomas
  • Biden covid symptoms continue to improve, White House says
  • The crypto boom runs on hype men like ‘BitBoy,’ an untrained Atlanta YouTuber
  • 5 economic forces behind the Fed’s next rate-hike decision
  • TikTokers are using calamine lotion as makeup primer. Doctors say: Bad idea.
  • Chips funding bill faces a final vote in Senate this week
  • For creators, community is the new follower count
  • How London Paid a Record Price to Dodge a Blackout
  • Mr. ‘Voltswagen’ Shocked Too Much. Can Mr. Congeniality Do Better?
  • Don’t Fear Italexit. It Won’t Happen.
  • Success Story Primark Is Ready to Shine on Its Own
  • How and Why China Is Centralizing Its Billion-Ton Iron Ore Trade
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • How Sri Lanka Landed in a Crisis and What It Means
  • Neither side is going to win the abortion fight with outlier cases
  • Quit, Joe, Quit! Biden could save the midterms with a one-term pledge.
  • Senators, pass the Respect for Marriage Act
  • As inmates languish, Prince George’s County judges turn a blind eye
  • Doctors and patients deserve a ‘conscience’ exception to abortion bans
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: JoongAng Daily: 25 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Foreign exchange transactions at Woori, Shinhan banks under investigation by prosecution

  • KBO sees biggest win ever as Tigers trounce Giants, 23-0

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.title: Guardian: 25 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live news: Kyiv warns Russian airstrikes will stall grain deal; Moscow speaks of ‘new era’ with Arab League
  • Ryanair warns new Covid-19 variants could disrupt air travel recovery this autumn – business live
  • France orders air-conditioned shops to save energy by shutting doors
  • For modern mothers, the toxic pull of the ‘momfluencer’ feels inescapable | Rhiannon Lucy Cosslett
  • Why reality TV loves an island
  • Dynasty is a horrid, crass, offensive show – so why can’t I stop binge-watching it? | Nancy Jo Sales
  • The 20 best easy Mediterranean recipes
  • Euro 2022’s legacy must be true parity for female footballers in England | Marie-Christine Bouchier

  • Is Murdoch tiring of Trump? Mogul’s print titles dump the ex-president
  • ‘Jailhouse Rock made prison seem like fun’: Billy Gibbons’s honest playlist

  • Diana Kennedy, influential guru of Mexican cuisine, dies at 99

  • Sydney airport ranked among world’s worst as delays persist: ‘Chaos doesn’t come close’
  • Bird flu outbreak on Farne Islands risks ‘unprecedented wildlife tragedy’
  • Plastic in the depths: how pollution took over our oceans
  • What the absurd class cosplay of Rishi Sunak and Liz Truss tells us about Britain | Nesrine Malik
  • ‘There’s never been a time when you could just say anything’: Frank Skinner on free speech, his bullying shame – and knob jokes
  • Dine like Da Vinci, unleash your inner diva – 101 ways the arts can slightly improve your life

  • Jonas Vingegaard takes Tour de France glory and Pogacar’s aura of invincibility
  • Lewis Hamilton hints he will prolong F1 stay after best finish of the season
  • A season of hope: Ukraine prepares for remarkable return of league football | Nick Ames
  • Brooke Henderson recovers from early four-putt to win Evian Championship
  • Ten of the best value-for-money signings so far in this transfer window | Niall McVeigh
  • De Kock dazzles before rain ruins England v South Africa ODI decider
  • Pope in Canada to apologise for abuse of Indigenous children in church schools
  • ‘It has been like hell’: life under fire in Donbas as war enters sixth month
  • Fran Kirby’s England rallying cry: ‘Let’s put a smile on people’s faces’
  • Monkeypox: Schiff demands to know why US does not have more vaccines
  • Viktor Orbán sparks outrage with attack on ‘race mixing’ in Europe

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 25 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 25 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-25 07:12AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Brother of late Hong Kong Canto-pop diva Anita Mui loses trademark lawsuit against biopic production company Edko Films
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong officials ‘actively considering’ cutting hotel quarantine to 3 or 4 days
  • Supporting Hong Kong students with special educational needs (SEN) – an estimated 57,000 children get help across local and international schools with the latter following voluntary guidelines
  • Hong Kong’s new environment minister pledges to move on green targets, start testing hydrogen powered cars by 2024
  • China wants Hong Kong to integrate with the Greater Bay Area, but can it convince young talent and employers it’s a good idea?
  • Rent control law fails to help tenants of Hong Kong’s subdivided flats, as most respondents to survey say they are no better protected than before
  • Hong Kong’s Urban Renewal Authority looks into feasibility of redeveloping 2 old areas in Kowloon City, cites planning challenges
  • Young hiker collapses and dies in Hong Kong as mercury hits 36.1 degrees Celsius, highest reading for July since records began
  • Hong Kong’s monetary base is strong enough to uphold US dollar peg despite recent capital outflows, finance chief says
  • Hong Kong’s MTR Corp apologises to family of elderly man found dead in station toilet hours after missing report filed, vows to investigate
  • Learn Chinese history to cultivate national identity and avoid being divided by foreign forces, Hong Kong security chief tells youngsters
  • China-Africa relations: Beijing’s ambassador to Burkina Faso works to make up ground on delayed projects
  • China’s AI ambitions are being propelled by 75-year-old Harvard grad Andrew Chi-Chih Yao through impact on start-ups
  • Rishi Sunak vows to get tough on China, Britain’s ‘biggest long-term threat’
  • Chinese and US scientists build bridges with cutting-edge Hale telescope project
  • US hypersonic missile test expected to spur on China’s programme
  • Chinese companies begin to embrace quantum technology
  • EU must treat Africa as an equal if it wishes to counter China’s influence on the continent
  • Chinese banking scandal: financial official placed under investigation
  • Former Huawei smartphone sub-brand Honor said to withdraw team from India amid geopolitical tensions
  • Taiwan left divided by US ex-defence chief’s call for longer compulsory military service
  • China’s social media giants to require influencers to disclose agency names on profile pages
  • China has become more ‘aggressive and dangerous’ to US, and its allies says chair of Joint Chiefs of Staff Mark Milley
  • Coronavirus: North Korea touts ‘Koryo’ traditional medicine to fight Covid-19 as critics cast doubts
  • City in Japan takes on marauding macaques after 42 people hurt in spate of monkey attacks
  • Why are the Philippine heirs of a sultan clashing with Malaysia over Sabah?
  • Myanmar executes democracy activists in first use of capital punishment for decades
  • Solomon Islanders hope ‘China can care for us’ as opinions shift amid Beijing-backed building spree
  • What’s behind the rise of Japan’s Sanseito, a far-right party that loves Trump and hates immigration?
  • Evacuations ordered as Japan’s Sakurajima volcano erupts, highest alert level raised
  • Singapore urged to arrest Sri Lanka’s Gotabaya Rajapaksa by South Africa-based human rights group
  • 3 dead in shooting at top Philippines university - ‘determined’ assassin had two guns
  • South Korea emerges as fastest-growing arms exporter in the world
  • Eyes on Donald Trump as he returns to Washington for first time since leaving White House
  • Russia charges dozens from Ukraine’s forces with ‘crimes against peace of humanity’
  • ‘Total BS’: Elon Musk denies affair with Google co-founder Sergey Brin’s wife
  • US semiconductor bill unites Bernie Sanders with conservative right in opposition to ‘corporate welfare’
  • 17 dead after boat carrying Haitian migrants sinks off Bahamas
  • Monkeypox ‘can be contained’ in US, White House doctor says after WHO triggers highest alert
  • I2U2: why US efforts to fan the flames of anti-China sentiment in West Asia, Middle East are failing
  • Major incident declared over large fire at English beauty spot
  • Pope lands in Canada, set for apologies to indigenous groups for abuse in Catholic schools
  • Joe Biden’s Covid-19 symptoms improve ‘significantly’, throat still sore
  • Russia’s Lavrov reassures Egypt on grain supplies amid Ukraine war
  • Quest to find US$181 million in bitcoin buried under 110,000 tons of rubbish
  • Ukraine war: ‘All Russia’s plans will fail’ when Kherson is ‘liberated’ by September, official says
  • Nearly 700 migrants found – at least 5 dead – in sea off Italy’s coast
  • Why Chinese buyers are still a force to reckon with in California’s property market
  • Goldman Sachs recalibrates MSCI China Index and earnings forecast amid new headwinds for the Chinese economy
  • Hang Seng hits one-week low before Fed rate meeting while Goldman trims targets on Chinese stocks
  • AI, machine learning tech promises US$600 billion annually for China economy as it pervades industries, says McKinsey
  • Hong Kong braces for increase in prime interest rate after imminent US Fed action to rein in 40-year-high inflation
  • Buy Chinese developers? Mortgage boycott suggests stock rebound is built on fragile confidence
  • Trending mobile app BeReal gets users to post a photo in a 2-minute window and has soared in popularity in 2022, despite glitches
  • Pretty Heart movie review: Jennifer Yu anchors gentle film that’s part education drama, part romantic comedy and part family melodrama
  • From Japanese knives to utensils for sushi and ramen, this Tokyo neighbourhood is a treasure trove for chefs and tourists
  • What is sleaze? Who is sleazy? Does it matter? A teenager with lust in her luggage heads to Alaska to find out
  • Climate anxiety: how to help young people cope – 84 per cent are worried about global warming and need adults’ support
  • Forget Kendall Jenner’s tequila and try sotol, raicilla or bacanora – 3 lesser known agave spirits
  • He finds Bollywood film locations and has visited 140 countries: meet Ramji Natarajan, the man behind the places you see on screen
  • 7 Korean actors heading to Hollywood in 2022: Blackpink’s Jennie and HoYeon Jung will act with Lily-Rose Depp, Cha Eun-woo has a gig with Rebel Wilson, and Park Seo-joon is in Disney’s The Marvels
  • Haute Joaillerie Edit: Messika’s new high jewellery worthy of an Egyptian queen – the Parisian brand’s evocative Beyond the Light collection draws on everything from hieroglyphs to the Eye of Horus
  • Haute Joaillerie Edit: Chaumet’s latest Ondes et Merveilles high jewellery collection explores marine marvels, featuring weighty precious stones from diamonds to Colombia emeralds and pink tourmalines
  • All of Donald Trump’s children with Melania Trump, Ivana Trump, Marla Maples: from Ivanka and Donald Jr. to Barron, Tiffany and Eric, how much do you really know about the former US president’s kids?
  • Inside the world’s most OTT superyachts: from IMAX theatres, helipads and hospitals, to ski rooms, golf driving ranges and priceless works of art by Leonardo da Vinci
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 25 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Monkeypox outbreak: WHO calls for public health measures to be stepped up
  • France's Eutelsat in talks over possible merger with UK satellite operator OneWeb
  • Thai car sales rise at slower pace in June; exports fall

  • US storm to another crushing 4x400m men's relay gold

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
